name: Application Ownership Abuse - User with Entra Role
description: Compromised user owns an application that has a high-privileged Entra ID role (Global Administrator).
long_description: A user account is the owner of an application registration. That application's service principal has been assigned the Global Administrator directory role. The attacker compromises the user, adds new credentials to the owned application, and authenticates as the service principal to escalate privileges.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_ownership_user_role:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationOwnershipAbuse
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Application Ownership Abuse - SP with Graph API
description: Compromised service principal owns an application with high-privileged Graph API permissions.
long_description: A service principal owns another application registration. The target application has AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All and RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory Graph API permissions. The attacker compromises the owning service principal (e.g., via exposed CI/CD credentials), adds credentials to the owned app, and uses the Graph API permissions to escalate.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_ownership_sp_api:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationOwnershipAbuse
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role:
      - 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All
      - 9e3f62cf-ca93-4989-b6ce-bf83c28f9fe8  # RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory

name: Application Ownership Abuse - Helpdesk Scenario
description: Helpdesk admin resets the password of a user who owns a privileged application.
long_description: A helpdesk administrator has the ability to reset passwords. One of the users whose password can be reset is the owner of an application with Application Administrator role. The attacker compromises the helpdesk admin, resets the app owner's password, then exploits the application ownership to escalate privileges.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_ownership_helpdesk:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationOwnershipAbuse
    scenario: helpdesk
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 9b895d92-2cd3-44c7-9d02-a6ac2d5ea5c3  # Application Administrator

name: Application Ownership Abuse - Group-Based
description: User inherits application ownership via security group membership.
long_description: A user is a member of a security group that owns an application with Global Administrator role. The attacker compromises the user, discovers group membership that grants application ownership, adds credentials to the owned app, and authenticates as the service principal.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_ownership_group:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationOwnershipAbuse
    assignment_type: group_member
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Application Administrator Abuse - Exchange Permissions
description: User with Application Administrator role targets an app with Exchange full mailbox access.
long_description: A user has the Application Administrator Entra ID role, which grants the ability to manage credentials for any application in the tenant. A target application has the Exchange Online full_access_as_app permission. The attacker adds credentials to the target app and gains full mailbox access across the organization.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_admin_user_exchange:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationAdministratorAbuse
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: exchange
    app_role: dc890d15-9560-4a4c-9b7f-a736ec74ec40  # full_access_as_app

name: Application Administrator Abuse - SP with Entra Role
description: Service principal with Application Administrator role targets an app with Global Administrator.
long_description: An automation service principal has the Application Administrator role. A target application's service principal has the Global Administrator role. The attacker compromises the automation SP, uses App Admin privileges to add credentials to the target app, and authenticates as Global Administrator.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_admin_sp_role:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationAdministratorAbuse
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Application Administrator Abuse - Group Ownership
description: User owns a group that has the Application Administrator role, enabling app credential management.
long_description: A user is the owner of a security group that has the Application Administrator role. A target application has RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory Graph API permission. The attacker compromises the user, adds themselves to the owned group, inherits Application Administrator role, and manages the target app's credentials.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_admin_group_owner:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationAdministratorAbuse
    assignment_type: group_owner
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role: 9e3f62cf-ca93-4989-b6ce-bf83c28f9fe8  # RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory

name: Application Administrator Abuse - Scoped to Application
description: Application Administrator role scoped to a specific application rather than tenant-wide.
long_description: A user has the Application Administrator role scoped to a specific application (not tenant-wide). That application has the Global Administrator role. The attacker compromises the user, manages credentials only for the scoped application, and authenticates as Global Administrator.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_admin_scoped:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationAdministratorAbuse
    scope: application
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Cloud App Administrator Abuse - User with Entra Role
description: User with Cloud Application Administrator role targets an app with Global Administrator.
long_description: A user has the Cloud Application Administrator Entra ID role. A target application has the Global Administrator role. Similar to Application Administrator abuse, but Cloud App Admin has slightly fewer privileges (cannot manage certain first-party apps). The attacker adds credentials to the target app and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  cloud_app_admin_user:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: CloudAppAdministratorAbuse
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Cloud App Administrator Abuse - Scoped
description: Cloud App Administrator role scoped to a specific application.
long_description: A user has the Cloud Application Administrator role scoped to a specific application. That application has the Global Administrator role. The attack is similar to the tenant-wide variant but limited to a single target application.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  cloud_app_admin_scoped:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: CloudAppAdministratorAbuse
    scope: application
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Key Vault Secret Theft - User Access
description: User with Key Vault access retrieves application client secrets to authenticate as a privileged app.
long_description: A user has Key Vault Contributor role on an Azure Key Vault that contains the client secret of a privileged application (with Global Administrator role). The attacker compromises the user, accesses the Key Vault, retrieves the client secret, and authenticates as the privileged application.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  kv_theft_user:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: KeyVaultSecretTheft
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Key Vault Secret Theft - Service Principal
description: Service principal with Key Vault access retrieves secrets to escalate via Graph API permissions.
long_description: A service principal has Key Vault Contributor role on a vault containing another application's client secret. The target application has AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All and Directory.ReadWrite.All Graph API permissions. The attacker compromises the SP, retrieves the secret, and authenticates as the target app.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  kv_theft_sp:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: KeyVaultSecretTheft
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role:
      - 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All
      - 19dbc75e-c2e2-444c-a770-ec69d8559fc7  # Directory.ReadWrite.All

name: Key Vault Secret Theft - Group-Based
description: User inherits Key Vault Contributor through security group membership.
long_description: A user is a member of a security group that has Key Vault Contributor role. The vault contains a client secret for an application with AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All permission. The attacker compromises the user, discovers group-inherited Key Vault access, retrieves the secret, and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  kv_theft_group:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: KeyVaultSecretTheft
    assignment_type: group_member
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role: 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All

name: Storage Certificate Theft - User Access
description: User with Storage Blob Data Reader downloads application certificates from blob storage.
long_description: A user has Storage Blob Data Reader role on a storage account containing certificate and private key files for a privileged application (with Global Administrator role). The attacker compromises the user, downloads the cert/key pair, and authenticates as the privileged application.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  storage_theft_user:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: StorageCertificateTheft
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Storage Certificate Theft - Service Principal
description: Service principal with storage access retrieves certificates to escalate via Graph API.
long_description: A service principal has Storage Blob Data Reader role on a storage account containing certificate files. The target application has AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All and Directory.ReadWrite.All Graph API permissions. The attacker compromises the SP, downloads the certs, and authenticates as the target app.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  storage_theft_sp:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: StorageCertificateTheft
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role:
      - 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All
      - 19dbc75e-c2e2-444c-a770-ec69d8559fc7  # Directory.ReadWrite.All

name: Storage Certificate Theft - Group-Based
description: User inherits Storage Blob Data Reader through security group membership.
long_description: A user is a member of a security group with Storage Blob Data Reader role. The storage account contains certificate files for a privileged application. The attacker compromises the user, discovers group-inherited storage access, downloads certificates, and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  storage_theft_group:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: StorageCertificateTheft
    assignment_type: group_member
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role: 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All

name: Managed Identity Theft - VM (User Access)
description: User with VM Contributor runs commands to extract managed identity token for Key Vault access.
long_description: A user has Contributor access to a virtual machine that has a system-assigned managed identity. The managed identity has access to a Key Vault containing secrets for a privileged application (with Graph API permissions). The attacker runs commands on the VM to extract the MI token, accesses Key Vault, and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  mi_theft_vm_user:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ManagedIdentityTheft
    source_type: vm
    target_resource_type: key_vault
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role:
      - 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All
      - 19dbc75e-c2e2-444c-a770-ec69d8559fc7  # Directory.ReadWrite.All

name: Managed Identity Theft - Logic App (SP Access)
description: Service principal modifies Logic App workflow to extract managed identity token for storage access.
long_description: A service principal has Contributor access to a Logic App with a system-assigned managed identity. The MI has access to a storage account containing certificates for a privileged application (with Global Administrator role). The attacker modifies the Logic App workflow to extract the MI token and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  mi_theft_logicapp_sp:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ManagedIdentityTheft
    source_type: logic_app
    target_resource_type: storage_account
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Managed Identity Theft - Function App (Certificate)
description: Modify Function App code to extract MI token and retrieve certificates from Key Vault.
long_description: An identity has Contributor access to a Function App with a system-assigned managed identity. The MI has access to a Key Vault containing certificate-based credentials for a privileged application. The attacker modifies the Function App code to extract the MI token, accesses Key Vault, and uses the certificate to escalate.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  mi_theft_functionapp_cert:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ManagedIdentityTheft
    source_type: function_app
    target_resource_type: key_vault
    credential_type: certificate
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role: 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All

name: Managed Identity Theft - Automation Account (Certificate)
description: Modify Automation Account runbook to extract MI token and download certificates from storage.
long_description: An identity has Contributor access to an Automation Account with a system-assigned managed identity. The MI has access to a storage account containing certificate-based credentials for a privileged application (with Global Administrator role). The attacker modifies a runbook to extract the MI token and escalates.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  mi_theft_automation_cert:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ManagedIdentityTheft
    source_type: automation_account
    target_resource_type: storage_account
    credential_type: certificate
    method: AzureADRole
    entra_role: 62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10  # Global Administrator

name: Managed Identity Theft - VM (Group-Based)
description: User inherits VM Contributor through group membership, extracts managed identity token.
long_description: A user is a member of a security group with Contributor access to a VM. The VM has a system-assigned managed identity with Key Vault access. The attacker compromises the user, discovers group-inherited VM access, runs commands to extract the MI token, and escalates via Key Vault secrets.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  mi_theft_vm_group:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ManagedIdentityTheft
    source_type: vm
    target_resource_type: key_vault
    assignment_type: group_member
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: graph
    app_role: 06b708a9-e830-4db3-a914-8e69da51d44f  # AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All

name: Application Ownership Abuse - SP with Exchange Access
description: Compromised service principal owns an application with Exchange full mailbox access.
long_description: A service principal owns another application that has Exchange Online full_access_as_app permission. The attacker compromises the owning SP, adds credentials to the owned application, and authenticates to gain full mailbox access across the organization.
yaml_content:
attack_paths:
  app_ownership_sp_exchange:
    enabled: true
    privilege_escalation: ApplicationOwnershipAbuse
    identity_type: service_principal
    method: APIPermission
    api_type: exchange
    app_role: dc890d15-9560-4a4c-9b7f-a736ec74ec40  # full_access_as_app
